title: androsace pubescens subsp. ciliata
intro: Androsace pubescens subsp. ciliata, also known as the hairy or ciliated rock-jasmine, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Primulaceae family, native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Androsace pubescens subsp. ciliata include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating various skin conditions and infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
